destinations.name: Satrangi Lake
destinations.description: Satrangi Lake, also known as the Seven Colored Lake, is a mesmerizing natural wonder located in the enchanting Naltar Valley of Gilgit-Baltistan. The lake is famed for its extraordinary display of seven distinct colors, which shift and blend with the changing light and weather conditions.
highlights: Seven distinct colors shifting with light and weather

destinations.name: Firoza Lake
destinations.description: Firoza Lake is a stunning high-altitude lake in Naltar Valley, surrounded by lush green meadows and towering mountains. It offers tranquility and opportunities for trekking and nature photography.
highlights: Surrounded by lush meadows and mountains
3–4 hour trek or jeep ride to reach
Ideal for nature lovers and photographers

destinations.name: Bireno Suspension Bridge
destinations.description: Bireno Suspension Bridge connects Danyore Valley to Karakoram University across the Hunza River. Built between 1957–1960 without proper engineering equipment, it stands as a testament to local ingenuity and cultural significance.
highlights: Connects Danyore Valley to Karakoram University

destinations.name: Taj Mughal Minar
destinations.description: Taj Mughal Minar, locally called 'Mughali Shikar', is a 700-year-old archaeological site in Gilgit City, built by Taj-ud-Din Mughal. It offers scenic views of the city and surrounding mountains.
highlights: Located on mountain ridge in Gilgit
Ideal for hikers and history enthusiasts

destinations.name: Naltar Valley
destinations.description: Naltar Valley is a stunning region at 3,000 meters’ elevation, known for snow-capped peaks, lush greenery, trekking trails, and lakes like Firoza Lake and Satrangi Lake.
highlights: Panoramic views of mountains and valleys
Trekking and winter sports at ski resort
Rich flora and fauna in forests
Includes lakes like Firoza, Satrangi, Blue and Bodlok

destinations.name: Chinese Cemetery
destinations.description: Chinese Cemetery in Danyore Valley is the final resting place of Chinese engineers and workers who died constructing the Karakoram Highway in the 1960s–70s.

destinations.name: Kutwal Lake
destinations.description: Kutwal Lake is a high-altitude lake in Haramosh Valley, surrounded by peaks like Haramosh, Laila, and Dobani. It is a serene destination for nature lovers.

destinations.name: Bagrot Valley
destinations.description: Bagrot Valley features alpine pastures, surrounded by peaks like Rakaposhi and Diran. Locals practice mountain agriculture, growing barley, maize, potatoes, and wheat.
highlights: Views of Rakaposhi, Diran, Dobani peaks

destinations.name: Kargah Buddha
destinations.description: Kargah Buddha is a 50-foot high cliff-face carving of Buddha from 4th–7th century, located 6 miles outside Gilgit city. It reflects the early Buddhist history of the region.

destinations.name: Junction of Three Mountain Ranges
destinations.description: The junction of Himalayas, Karakoram, and Hindu-Kush near Bunji creates a unique confluence of mountain ranges, enhancing the region's geographical significance.
highlights: Confluence of Himalayas, Karakoram, and Hindu-Kush

destinations.name: Mazino Pass
destinations.description: The Mazino Pass, situated approximately 42 kilometers from Astore's administrative center, is a renowned trekking destination and base camp for climbers attempting Nanga Parbat, the 9th highest peak globally.
highlights: High-altitude trekking route (5,377m) linking Rupal Valley to Diamer region.
Offers stunning alpine scenery and solitude.
Strategically important for mountaineers targeting Nanga Parbat.

destinations.name: Burzil Pass
destinations.description: Burzil Pass, at 13,808 ft, is a historic caravan route linking Srinagar to Gilgit, passing near the Line of Control in Kashmir. It is the oldest known route connecting Gilgit to Skardu via Deosai Plateau.
highlights: Verdant summit with alpine grasses in summer.

destinations.name: Kala Pani
destinations.description: Kalapani Valley is known for lush green meadows, streams, and towering mountains, offering a serene retreat in Astore District for nature lovers and trekkers.

destinations.name: Dirlay Lake
destinations.description: Dirlay Lake is nestled in Dirlay Valley, known for pristine streams, rivers, and serpentine-shaped lake waters, surrounded by towering mountains.

destinations.name: Allah Wali Jheel
destinations.description: Allah Wali Jheel in Perishing Valley features a natural formation of the Arabic script for 'Allah' in its landscape, set amidst lush green fields and towering mountains.

destinations.name: Domail Rainbow Lake
destinations.description: Domail Rainbow Lake in Minimarg is a stunning lake covered with lush meadows and flowers, sometimes hidden by fog or rain, making it a lucky sight for visitors.

destinations.name: Rama Lake
destinations.description: Rama Lake, near Nanga Parbat, is a high-altitude lake surrounded by lush meadows and pine/cedar forests, popular for trekking and adventure tourism.
highlights: Near Nanga Parbat, the ninth-highest mountain.
Surrounded by meadows and dense forests.

destinations.name: Deosai National Park
destinations.description: Deosai National Park, at 4,114 m, is the second-highest plateau in the world, famous for Himalayan Marmots, Brown Bears, and Sheosar Lake.
highlights: Diverse wildlife including Brown Bear and Himalayan Marmots.

destinations.name: Rakaposhi
destinations.description: Rakaposhi is a prominent mountain in the Karakoram range standing 7,788 meters tall. It offers stunning views of Nagar Valley and is home to endangered species like Marco Polo sheep, snow leopards, and brown bears.
highlights: Uninterrupted 6,000-meter descent from summit to base.
Rakaposhi View Point offers closest view of the mountain.
Scenic Karakoram Highway with cultural and archaeological spots.

destinations.name: Sikandarabad Valley
destinations.description: Sikandarabad is the administrative center of Nagar District, located along the Karakoram Highway near the base of Rakaposhi mountain.
highlights: Picturesque village nestled in Karakoram Range.
Close proximity to Rakaposhi base camp.

destinations.name: Kacheli Lake
destinations.description: Kacheli Lake is a high-altitude lake in Miacher Valley, known for its untouched natural beauty.
highlights: Ideal for trekking and nature photography.

destinations.name: Rush Lake
destinations.description: Rush Lake is one of Pakistan’s highest lakes at 4,694 meters, offering breathtaking scenery and pristine waters.

destinations.name: Hispar Glacier
destinations.description: Hispar Glacier stretches 49 km, connecting to Snow Lake, making it a paradise for trekkers and adventure enthusiasts.
highlights: Trek from Biafo Glacier to Hispar Glacier.
Access to Snow Lake frozen glacial area.

destinations.name: Natural Cricket Stadium
destinations.description: The world's highest natural Cricket Stadium, located at Pissan Valley, Nagar District, offering scenic surroundings and a lush green pitch.
highlights: Altitude of 8,500 feet above sea level.
Located under Rakaposhi and Diran peaks.
Hosts local cricket tournaments and promotes tourism.

destinations.name: Skardu
destinations.description: LAND OF PEACE District Skardu in Baltistan Division is located at the confluence of Indus River and Shayok River that leads toward the legendary tourist destination of the greatest mountain peaks of the world. Skardu Valley is path to some of the highest mountain peaks in the world such as Gasherbrum, K2, and K3. This region is the manifestation of hospitality and respect for tourists.
highlights: Languages: Balti, English, and Urdu widely spoken.
Famous Tourist Spots: Kharpocho Fort, Manthal Buddha Rock, Sadpara and Kachura Lakes, Shangrila Lake.
Katpana Sand Dunes (cold desert) and Hargisa streams.
Popular Activities: Fishing, Boating, Trekking, Rock Climbing, Trophy Hunting, Paragliding, Skiing, Mountain Biking.

destinations.name: Deosai National Park
destinations.description: Deosai is a high-altitude alpine plain at 4,114 m, famous for Himalayan Marmots, Brown Bears, wildflowers, and Sheosar Lake. Located between Skardu and Astore districts, it offers trekking, mountaineering, camping, and fishing.
highlights: Second highest plateau in the world.
Sheosar Lake offers breathtaking scenery with wildflowers and butterflies.

destinations.name: Chunda Valley
destinations.description: Chunda Valley is a picturesque valley near Skardu city at 2,500 meters, known for panoramic mountain views, high-altitude lakes, and trekking trails.
highlights: One hour drive from Skardu downtown.
Features Chunda Fort and high-altitude lakes.
Best visited in spring from late February to April.

destinations.name: Basho Valley
destinations.description: Basho Valley, at 3,600 meters, is known for its grapes, snow-capped mountains, Basho Glacier, and wildlife including deer, markhor, ibex, and snow leopards.
highlights: Known for scenic landscapes and wildlife.

destinations.name: Nangsoq Valley
destinations.description: Nangsoq is Pakistan's first organic village, promoting traditional farming practices and Baltistan cuisine. Located near Kharpocho Fort.
highlights: Ideal for excursions and cultural tourism.
Visited by Prince Charles and Duchess of Cornwall.

destinations.name: Sadpara Lake
destinations.description: Sadpara Lake is one of the largest freshwater lakes in Pakistan, supplying water to Skardu City and popular for trout fishing and boating.

destinations.name: Katpana Cold Desert
destinations.description: Katpana Cold Desert, also called Nakpo Biama, is a high-altitude desert with vast sand dunes sometimes covered in snow during winter.
highlights: One of the highest deserts in the world.

destinations.name: Manthal Buddha Rock
destinations.description: Manthal Buddha Rock is a historical heritage site with an 8th-century Buddha sculpture and inscriptions in Balti language.

destinations.name: Kachura Lakes
destinations.description: Kachura Lakes consist of Upper and Lower Kachura Lakes (Shangrila Lake) and are major attractions in Skardu, ideal for boating and leisure.

destinations.name: Kharphocho Fort
destinations.description: Kharpocho Fort, the 'King of Forts', is an ancient fort built between 1490-1505 by the Maqpoon dynasty and overlooks Skardu town.
highlights: Important historical and cultural heritage site.
Strategically built on a hill overlooking Skardu.

destinations.name: Shigar
destinations.description: LAND OF MOUNTAINS Shigar is the gateway to the Karakoram, with fertile lands irrigated by the Shigar River. Heritage sites include the 400-year-old Shigar Fort and Amburiq Mosque, recognized for restoration and UNESCO conservation awards.
highlights: Gateway to Karakoram and Baltoro Glacier.
Fertile valley producing grapes, peaches, pears, walnuts, and apricots.
Close proximity to Skardu (approx. 30 minutes drive).

destinations.name: K-2 The Second Highest Mountain on Earth
destinations.description: K-2 is the 2nd highest mountain in the world at 8,611 meters, located in the Karakoram Range on the Pak-China border. Skardu serves as the gateway, with trekking routes passing through Shigar, Askole, and Concordia over Baltoro Glacier. First summit was achieved in 1954 by Italian climbers Achille Compagnoni and Lino Lacedelli.
highlights: Nickname: Killer Mountain due to climbing difficulty and fatalities.
Local name: Chogori in Balti language.
Trekking permits regulated by Gilgit-Baltistan government.

destinations.name: Blind Lake
destinations.description: Blind Lake (Jarba Zhou Lake) is a scenic lake near Shigar, about 30 minutes from Skardu. Located on the K-2 trekking route, it offers walks along sand, rocks, and lush grass, set amid mountains and cold desert surroundings.
highlights: Located in the legendary K-2 route.

destinations.name: Sarfaranga Cold Desert
destinations.description: Sarfaranga Cold Desert at 7,500 feet in Shigar District is surrounded by mountains and the Indus River. The desert hosts the annual Sarfaranga Jeep Rally and adventure activities such as free-style polo, boating, rock climbing, and cultural performances.
highlights: Highest cold desert in the world.
Annual Jeep Rally promotes adventure tourism.
Unique sunset views over sand dunes.

destinations.name: Ghanche
destinations.description: LAND OF TOURISM Ghanche is the easternmost district of Gilgit-Baltistan. Known for its rich cultural heritage, historical sites, and local festivals, it features architectural masterpieces like Khaplu Palace and Chaqchan Mosque. Popular activities include trekking, camping, and trout fishing.
highlights: Khaplu Palace and Chaqchan Mosque reflect traditional craftsmanship.
Local dances: Mandoq Taimo and Ragi Taimo.
Gateway to Masherbrum and Hushe Valley treks.

destinations.name: Keris Valley
destinations.description: Gateway to Ghanche district, known for lush fields, historic houses, the 'Door to Heaven' pedestrian bridge, and centuries-old hydropower and canal systems. Warm hospitality and beautiful landscapes make it ideal for summer visits.
highlights: Astana of Hazrat Mir Mukhtar and Ali Mosavi.
Confluence of Indus and Shyok Rivers.

destinations.name: Barah Valley
destinations.description: Located along the Shyok River, 20 minutes from Khaplu. Known for its serene landscape, fish farms, natural springs, and tranquil environment. One of the lesser-known gems of Khaplu.
highlights: Famous lake at the top of the valley.

destinations.name: Haldi Cones
destinations.description: Granite peaks visible from Khaplu, Yuchung Masherbrum viewpoint, and Machulu village. Haldi village is the closest access point for trekking and sightseeing.
highlights: Close to Haldi village and Khaplu town.

destinations.name: Chorbat Valley
destinations.description: Part of the Shyok River valley spanning Pakistan and India. Historically significant with settlements in both countries. Extends from Khaplu to Chalunka village in Nubra.
highlights: Split between Pakistan-administered Ghanche and Indian-administered Ladakh.

destinations.name: Kharfaq Lake
destinations.description: High-altitude lake in Kharfaq Valley, famous for trout and indigenous fish species. Surrounded by mountains, glaciers, and pristine streams.
highlights: Best visited in July and August.

destinations.name: Ghanche Lake
destinations.description: Known as 'Khenpi Tso', at 4,600 meters above sea level, surrounded by lush pastures, glaciers, and views of multiple 8,000+ meter peaks including K2, Broad Peak, and Gasherbrum series.
highlights: Panoramic views of world's highest mountains.

destinations.name: Chaqchan Mosque
destinations.description: Dating back to 1370, one of the oldest mosques in Baltistan. Features a blend of Tibetan, Mughal, and Persian architecture. Built on an old Buddhist site by Syed Amir Kabir Ali Hamdani.
highlights: Double-story structure with winter prayer rooms.
Verandah on all sides of the top floor.

destinations.name: Gondogoro Pass
destinations.description: Mountain pass at 5,585 meters connecting Gondogoro Glacier to Vigne Glacier. Popular among trekkers and climbers; challenging slopes and avalanche risks require experience and crampons.
highlights: Access to Hushe Valley and K2 Base Camp.
Steep snow slopes and rockfall hazards.
Route established in 1986 linking Concordia to Hushe.

destinations.name: Khaplu Fort
destinations.description: Also known as Yabgo Khar, mid-19th century palace and fort in Khaplu. Fusion of Himalayan, Kashmiri, and Mughal architecture. Semi-octagonal timber entrance and balconies enhance heritage value.
highlights: Royal residence of Raja of Khaplu.

destinations.name: Khaplu Valley
destinations.description: Administrative and commercial center of Ghanche. Gateway to Hushe Valley, lakes, peaks, and glaciers. Cultural richness, fruit orchards, and restored heritage hotels like Serena Khaplu Fort attract tourists.
highlights: Heritage hotels serving local Balti dishes.
Vibrant fruit orchards and high mountains.

destinations.name: Saling Valley
destinations.description: Picturesque valley offering high-altitude landscapes, camping beside a lake, springs, and a royal history. Fertile land grows wheat, potatoes, and vegetables. Known for hospitality and low crime rate.
highlights: Fish farm and hot spring nearby.
Former royal estate with historical significance.

destinations.name: Hushe Valley
destinations.description: Last village of Ghanche, surrounded by high Karakoram peaks. Epicenter for trekking and mountaineering, with access to Gondogoro Pass, Gasherbrum peaks, and K2. Popular among climbers and trekkers worldwide.
highlights: Access point for trekking to K2 and Gasherbrum.

destinations.name: Thalley Valley
destinations.description: Lush green valley 35 km from district headquarters. Offers camping, hiking, tracks to K2 base camp and Shigar Valley. Milky moonlit nights provide unforgettable views, with snow-capped mountains in the backdrop.
highlights: Track access to Shigar Valley and K2 Base Camp.

destinations.name: Manthokha Waterfall
destinations.description: Manthokha Waterfall is a stunning natural attraction in Kharmang Valley, dropping 180 feet amid lush greenery. Perfect for picnics, photography, and nature lovers.
highlights: Ideal spot for picnics and photography.
Opportunities for fishing and trout farming.

destinations.name: Khamush Waterfall
destinations.description: Khamush Waterfall is a breathtaking natural wonder in Kharmang Valley, surrounded by scenic mountains and lush greenery. A must-visit for nature enthusiasts.
highlights: Ideal for adventurers and nature lovers.

destinations.name: Ghandus Lake
destinations.description: Ghandus Lake is a hidden gem at 2,800 meters above sea level, offering breathtaking views with turquoise waters and a mountain backdrop.
highlights: Ideal for hiking, trekking, and camping.

destinations.name: Tolti Valley
destinations.description: Tolti Valley, the administrative capital of Kharmang, offers lush greenery, rivers, and a rich cultural heritage. Historically a trade route between Skardu and Kargil.

destinations.name: Olding Valley
destinations.description: Olding Valley, the last village of Kharmang, is known for mountains, lush fields, and the Indus River. Rich cultural heritage with apple and fruit orchards.

destinations.name: Mehdiabad Valley
destinations.description: Mehdiabad Valley, formerly Parkuta, is a beautiful valley comprising Serling, Doqbar, Mehdiabad Khas, Bedong, and Pandah Village.

destinations.name: Pari Valley
destinations.description: Pari Valley is known for its stunning suspension bridge and Saspolo apples. The valley combines natural beauty with unique local produce.
highlights: Renowned Saspolo apples unique to the valley.

destinations.name: Saspolo Apple
destinations.description: Saspolo Apple is unique to Kharmang’s Pari Valley, grown with traditional methods and celebrated in the annual Saspolo Festival.
highlights: Grown without artificial fertilizers or pesticides.
Supports local economy and cultural heritage.

destinations.name: Diamer
destinations.description: LAND OF TRADITIONS District Diamer is gateway to Gilgit-Baltistan, rich in prehistoric rock carvings, inscriptions, and petroglyphs. It also hosts the Diamer-Bhasha Dam and Nanga Parbat, attracting tourists and adventure seekers.
highlights: Famous Valleys: Tangir, Darel, Chilas, Bunar Das, Gonar Farm, Raikot.
Fairy Meadows is a major attraction.
Popular Activities: Mountaineering, trekking, cultural tourism.

destinations.name: Darel Valley
destinations.description: Darel Valley lies along the Darel River and is known for its beautiful autumn scenery, fertile patches among barren mountains, and small villages like Gayel.
highlights: River roads with scenic views and scattered trees.

destinations.name: Chilas Valley
destinations.description: Chilas is the divisional capital of Diamer-Astore Division, strategically along the Indus River and connected to the ancient Silk Road. It features Buddhist monasteries and historical sites.
highlights: Old city with Indus Valley Civilization links.
Mix of Kohistani and Dardic peoples.

destinations.name: Thalpan Petroglyphs
destinations.description: Thalpan Rock Inscriptions feature over 50,000 carvings and 5,000 inscriptions, dating back 9000 years, and are considered one of the largest rock art provinces in the world.
highlights: Declared significant by World Watch Monuments, New York.

destinations.name: Babusar Pass
destinations.description: Babusar Pass at 4,173 m connects KP with Gilgit-Baltistan via Chilas Valley on the Karakoram Highway. Known for wildflowers, thin forests, and steep gradients.
highlights: Highest point in Babusar Valley accessible by car.
Route closed Oct–June due to snow.

destinations.name: Fairy Meadows
destinations.description: Fairy Meadows, locally known as Joot, is grassland at the base of Nanga Parbat. A National Park declared in 1995, it is a trekking hub and offers camping and breathtaking alpine views.
highlights: Launch point for summiting Nanga Parbat’s Raikot face.
Accessed via 15 km jeep-able trek from Raikot Bridge.
Considered one of the most dangerous roads in the world.

destinations.name: Nanga Parbat
destinations.description: Nanga Parbat (8,126 m) is the 9th highest mountain in the world, also called the 'Naked Mountain'. Known for its difficulty, it is a major attraction for mountaineers and trekkers.
highlights: Nickname: Killer Mountain due to high fatalities.
Local Tibetan name: Diamer or Deo Mir.

destinations.name: Burst Valley
destinations.description: Burst Valley is located in Ghizer District of Gilgit-Baltistan. The valley's stunning natural beauty, including scenic landscapes and trout streams, makes it a popular spot for nature lovers and anglers. Its proximity to Shandur Top, known for the annual polo festival, adds to its appeal.
highlights: Close to Shandur Top and annual polo festival

destinations.name: Shandur Pass
destinations.description: Shandur Pass is famous for the highest polo ground in the world. A twisting 61 km road from Gupis Valley passes lush green fields and forests, offering mesmerizing views along the way.
highlights: Highest polo ground in the world

destinations.name: Khalti Lake
destinations.description: Khalti Lake is situated in Khalti village at an altitude of about 2217 m. Known for its trout habitat, the lake usually freezes in winter. It hosts the annual Khalti Ice Hockey Festival in January.

destinations.name: Phandar Valley
destinations.description: Phandar Valley, known as 'Little Kashmir', is famous for lush green fields, deep blue lakes, and the river flowing across it. Easily accessible from Gilgit and Chitral, it offers peaceful landscapes and trout fishing.
highlights: Lush green fields and deep blue lakes

destinations.name: Yasin Valley
destinations.description: Yasin Valley is a high mountain valley in Hindu Kush, popular for scenic beauty and trekking. It hosts the annual Tukhm Rezi festival and traditional polo matches.
highlights: High mountain valley in Hindu Kush
Annual Tukhm Rezi festival and traditional polo matches

destinations.name: Karumber Lake
destinations.description: KarUmber Lake is one of the most pristine lakes of Pakistan at an elevation of 4272 m. It lies on the boundary of Broghil Valley in Chitral and Ishkoman Valley in Gilgit-Baltistan.
highlights: Boundary of Broghil and Ishkoman Valleys

destinations.name: Ishkoman Valley
destinations.description: The Ishkoman Valley lies in the north of Ghizer District bordering Afghanistan and the Pamir Wakhan Corridor. Famous for glaciers, trekking, and scenic remote locations.

destinations.name: Gahkuch
destinations.description: Gahkuch is the capital of Ghizer at a distance of 72 km from Gilgit City. Surrounded by mountains along the Gilgit River, it offers lush green valleys and accommodations for tourists.
highlights: Lush green valley along Gilgit River

destinations.name: Chatorkhand
destinations.description: Chatorkhand is the main town in Ishkoman Valley along the Karambar River. It is a deep valley with high mountains, lush greenery, and snow-capped peaks in the distance.

destinations.name: Borith Lake
destinations.description: Borith Lake is a hamlet in the surroundings of the Borith Lake to the northwest of Husseini, near Ghulkin, Gojal, in Upper Hunza. Historically a hunting spot for the Mir of Hunza, the lake is an important bird sanctuary for migrating wildfowl. Its size has reduced over the years due to receding glaciers and climate change.
highlights: Historic hunting place of Mir of Hunza
destinations.best_time_to_visit: March to June & September to November

destinations.name: Passu Glacier
destinations.description: Passu Glacier is situated south of Passu village in Hunza. Linked with Batura Glacier in the Batura Muztagh range, Passu Glacier lies beneath the iconic Passu Peak.

destinations.name: Khunjerab Pass
destinations.description: Khunjerab Pass, also called the 'Gateway to China', is one of the world's highest international border crossings at 4,693 meters (15,397 ft). It connects Hunza District with China's Xinjiang region.

destinations.name: Sost Valley
destinations.description: Sost is the last Pakistani town on the Karakoram Highway before the China border. It serves as a Dry Port and customs point for trade, and is known for its peaceful environment and hospitable local people.
highlights: Last Pakistani town on KKH before China

destinations.name: Kilik Pass and Mintaka Pass
destinations.description: Kilik and Mintaka Passes are historically significant caravan routes of the Silk Road connecting Pakistan with China and Afghanistan. They lie north of Misgar village.

destinations.name: Misgar Valley
destinations.description: Misgar Valley lies near the borders with China and Afghanistan. It is home to the Mintika and Kilik passes, and the historic Kalam Darchi Fort built by the British to monitor the region.

destinations.name: Karimabad Valley
destinations.description: Karimabad, formerly Baltit, is a cultural hub of Hunza famous for Baltit Fort and the scenic Hunza Valley. It is known for stunning landscapes and local hospitality.

destinations.name: Kalamdarchi Fort
destinations.description: Built in 1932-1933 during British rule, Kalamdarchi Fort was used to monitor incursions from China and Afghanistan. It served as a base for Gilgit Scouts and later Pak Army TPS.
highlights: Historic fort built during British rule

destinations.name: Baltit Fort
destinations.description: Baltit Fort was the capital of Hunza State and home of the royal family for 700 years. Renovated by Aga Khan Trust, it offers panoramic views of Hunza Valley and historical architecture.

destinations.name: Altit Fort
destinations.description: Altit Fort, over 900 years old, overlooks the Hunza River and showcases the rich cultural heritage of the Baltit people. One of the oldest surviving forts in the area.

destinations.name: Aliabad Hunza
destinations.description: Aliabad is the administrative and commercial center of Hunza District. Located along the Karakoram Highway, it lies between two forks of the Hunza River and offers scenic views.

destinations.name: Shimshal Lake
destinations.description: Shimshal Lake lies near the highest village in Upper Hunza at ~13,000 meters above sea level. Fed by glacier melt, it provides nesting grounds for migratory birds and is known for breathtaking scenery.
highlights: Highest village region in Upper Hunza

destinations.name: Attabad Lake
destinations.description: Attabad Lake formed in 2010 due to a landslide in Gojal Valley, Hunza. The lake is famous for boating, jet skiing, and recreational activities, with stunning turquoise waters surrounded by Karakoram peaks.
